# CANDIDATE: AGGRESSIVE_PREMIUM_STOP_SWEEP

**Filed:** 2026-06-18  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** parameter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We hypothesize that the current premium stop of -10% for the aggressive account baseline may be too tight, causing premature exits (false stops) on winning trades in the OOS sample. By testing a range of stop percentages from -6% to -15%, we aim to identify a threshold that reduces false stops on winners while maintaining adequate protection on losers, thereby improving edge capture on the J anchor days and improving walk-forward and out-of-sample performance.

## Mechanism

The aggressive account baseline uses: block_level_rejection=True, block_elite_bull=True (VIX 15-17.5), premium_stop_pct_bear=-0.10, tp1_qty_fraction=0.667, runner=5x, time_stop=20min, risk_cap=50%, vix_bear_threshold=15.0, vix_bull_max=30.0. We will test premium_stop_pct_bear across [-0.06, -0.07, -0.08, -0.09, -0.10, -0.12, -0.15] using run_backtest(). For each threshold, we measure: false stop count on the 13 winners in the OOS n=27 set (per task), walk-forward (WF) ratio, OOS delta vs baseline (-0.10), and edge capture on J anchor days. We select the threshold that minimizes false stops on winners while maximizing edge capture and WF ratio, subject to not increasing losses on J loser days beyond baseline.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** Aggressive account baseline assumes $25K+ for qty=28; $1K paper account scales to ~14% of headline P&L.
2. **Sample bias:** OOS n=27 set is a walk-forward held-out window (selection method unspecified). Testing 7 thresholds poses overfit risk; we use WF ratio to guard against overfit.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (will compute WF ratio and OOS delta for each threshold post-backtest)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (no realistic fill validation on top 3 J days for any threshold)
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: Too loose a stop (e.g., -0.06) may increase losses on bearish days if stop not triggered.
   - Max drawdown: Could increase if stop too loose and larger losses occur on adverse moves.
   - Blow-up: Sequence of losses from loose stop combined with 50% risk cap could cause significant drawdown.
6. **Concentration:** NEEDS-DATA (concentration for selected threshold unknown pending backtest)

## Pre-merge gate

- Gym validators must pass for selected threshold.
- Walk-forward analysis must show WF ratio >= 0.70 for selected threshold.
- Real-fills validation on top 3 J days must show <20% difference between BS-sim and realistic fills for selected threshold.
- Selected threshold must not increase losses on J loser days beyond baseline (engine loss on J losers <= current baseline loss).

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- No backtest data to support hypothesis. Task references OOS n=27 with 12 losers at avg -219, but winner data and false-stop counts unknown. Proposing sweep to gather data.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

No conflict with existing candidates; parameter change for aggressive account baseline (Gamma-Bold account per dual-account rules). May complement watchers/filters by providing better stop setting. Does not affect Gamma-Safe account.
